x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces ACS's thin machine-readable footprint (the Community Partner provider directory and its aggregate child-welfare, prevention, foster-care, and youth-justice reports) and adds report_child_care_concern — the net-new write workflow ACS delegates today to NYC 311. Case data stays confidential; suspected abuse is always redirected to the NY State Central Register.

server.name: io.nyc.acs
server.title: NYC Administration for Children's Services (ACS)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Administration for Children's Services — the ACS Community Partner provider directory plus aggregate accountability statistics on child welfare, preventive services, foster care, and youth & family justice from NYC Open Data, plus the net-new ability to submit and track a complaint about a child care provider that today is delegated to NYC 311. ACS data is confidential by statute: only aggregates are exposed, never an individual case, child, or family.
server.instructions: Use find_providers / get_provider to locate ACS Community Partner programs (family support, prevention, enrichment) by borough, service type, or council district — this is the only address-level ACS data. find_child_welfare_indicators, find_prevention_services, find_foster_care_statistics, and find_juvenile_justice_statistics return AGGREGATE counts and rates by reporting period and geography ONLY — ACS never exposes an individual investigation, foster child, or youth. report_child_care_concern (net-new) submits a complaint about a child care PROVIDER; it models the provider concern, not case data. CRITICAL: if the user describes a child being abused or neglected, do NOT collect case details here — direct them to call the NY State Central Register at 1-800-342-3720 (911 if a child is in immediate danger). Confirm the provider, category, and description before submitting a complaint.

tools.name: find_providers
tools.title: Find Providers
tools.description: Search ACS Community Partner providers/programs by borough, service type, active status, council district, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_provider
tools.title: Get Provider
tools.description: Fetch a single ACS Community Partner provider (program, agency, address, hours, contact, geography) by program code.

tools.name: find_child_welfare_indicators
tools.title: Find Child Welfare Indicators
tools.description: Search AGGREGATE child-welfare indicators (SCR intakes, investigations, indicated/unsubstantiated findings, emergency removals, referrals to prevention) by category, indicator, borough, or year. Never returns an individual case.

tools.name: find_prevention_services
tools.title: Find Prevention Services
tools.description: Search AGGREGATE preventive-services measures (children served, new cases, utilization, case-closure outcomes) by metric, borough, or year. Never a per-family record.

tools.name: find_foster_care_statistics
tools.title: Find Foster Care Statistics
tools.description: Search AGGREGATE foster-care statistics (children in 24-hour care, placements by CD, permanency outcomes, graduation rates, educational continuity, government ID, psychiatric medication) by metric, borough, or year. Never an individual child.

tools.name: find_juvenile_justice_statistics
tools.title: Find Youth Justice Statistics
tools.description: Search AGGREGATE youth & family justice statistics (detention admissions by CD, detention/placement demographics, incident reports, pre-placement, abuse-allegation detention) by metric, borough, or year. Never an individual youth.

tools.name: list_my_complaints
tools.title: List My Complaints
tools.description: List the authenticated submitter's own child care provider complaints and their status — today handled outside ACS by NYC 311.

tools.name: get_complaint
tools.title: Get Complaint
tools.description: Fetch a single child care provider complaint by id.

tools.name: report_child_care_concern
tools.title: Report a Child Care Concern
tools.description: Submit a complaint about a CHILD CARE PROVIDER (health, safety, staffing, licensing, quality). NET-NEW workflow with no ACS-owned API today — it is delegated to NYC 311. This models a provider concern, not case data. CRITICAL: this is NOT for reporting child abuse or neglect — if a child is being harmed, redirect the user to the NY State Central Register at 1-800-342-3720 (or 911 for immediate danger) and set childInvolved=true to trigger that redirect. Mutates state — confirm provider, category, and description first.

x-agent-skills.note: Each tool maps to one of the ten NYC common government-process Agent Skills via x-agent-skill.

prompts.name: explore_acs
prompts.description: Search and read NYC Children's Services (ACS) records — Providers, ChildWelfare, Prevention, FosterCare.

prompts.name: report_acs
prompts.description: ChildCareComplaint — submit a complaint about a child care provider (models the provider concern, never case data; abuse routed to the State Central Register)

resources.name: NYC Children's Services (ACS) — profile
resources.description: NYC Children's Services (ACS): 9 operations, 9 tools, verb “insource”.

resources.name: Providers record (templated)
resources.description: A single NYC Children's Services (ACS) Providers record by id.
